description_long: This is a rustic stone water well with a cylindrical stone base and a wooden canopy supported by four wooden posts. The roof is made of weathered wooden planks arranged in a pitched, gabled style, with some planks missing or misaligned, giving it an aged appearance. A metal crank handle is attached to one side, suggesting a manual mechanism for drawing water, though no bucket or rope is visible. The stonework of the well is rough and uneven, with visible seams and a slightly mossy texture. A loose wooden plank lies on the ground beside the well, possibly once part of the roof or structure. The overall look is utilitarian and worn, suitable for an outdoor urban or park setting.
description: A weathered stone water well with a wooden gabled roof, metal crank handle, and rustic, aged features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden roof with missing planks, the metal crank handle on the right, and the open stone well mouth.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the triangular wooden roof structure, the absence of a bucket, and the loose plank on the ground.
description_view_2: Rear view with a clearer look at the open well, the uneven roof planks, and the crank handle on the left.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wooden support structure, the gabled roof, and the stone well's circular form.
attribute_well_cover_design: No full cover; partial wooden gabled roof with missing and misaligned planks, made of weathered wood.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Metal crank handle present, but no visible pulley or bucket.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Stone wall encircles the well opening, approximately waist-high, serving as a protective barrier.
